critic.verdict: Perfectly timed but lacks the complexity and lane variety required for an Overdrive chart, relying on highly repetitive 3-lane sweeps.

issues.problem: Highly repetitive GAP -> NOTE -> BAR pattern repeats 7 times consecutively, causing the segment to feel very monotonous.
A nearly unbroken 24-beat quarter-note L-C-R sweep is used. This is far too simple and physically repetitive for an Overdrive difficulty.
The chart ends abruptly at 31.2s, leaving the final outro section (bars 18-19) completely empty, which feels anticlimactic.

design_notes: S0: Slowly introduces the synth textures with steady, spaced-out taps alternating between the outer GAP and NOTE lanes, anchored by a central hold. S1: Marking the drop's arrival, the speed increases to a dense sequence of 8th notes weaving left-to-right to match the rising melodic line. S2: Maintains the peak energy of the drop by alternating lanes on 8th notes, throwing in rhythmic holds that challenge coordination with single off-lane taps. S3: The track enters a calmer phase, prompting a spacious layout of 4-on-the-floor kicks on the center BAR lane and occasional side accents to let the player breathe. S4: A brief final outro that tapers off quickly, concluding the chart with a satisfying single-note resolution.

design_sections.name: S0
design_sections.intent: Intro with a slow build-up, introducing the BAR lane hold and comfortable alternating taps on the outer lanes.

design_sections.name: S1
design_sections.intent: The drop starts with a high-intensity stream of alternating notes across all three lanes, building momentum.

design_sections.name: S2
design_sections.intent: Main body of the drop, keeping high energy with 0.5 beat intervals, incorporating rhythmic holds and playful taps during holds.

design_sections.name: S3
design_sections.intent: Break section with a lower energy profile, allowing the player to breathe with sparse 1.0 beat spacing before a final hold sequence.

design_sections.name: S4
design_sections.intent: The outro winds down cleanly, culminating in a single final note on the NOTE lane.
